# Figure description: Картограмма_геологической_изученности.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Картограмма_геологической_изученности.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index/coverage cartogram** (картограмма геологической изученности) — a meta-map showing the spatial extent and scale of previous geological survey work carried out over a region. It is not a geological map per se, but rather a **geological survey coverage diagram** at 1:1,000,000 scale, documenting which map sheets have been covered by surveys of different scales and types.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a large area of **northern Kazakhstan**, encompassing portions of **map sheet rows M-42 and N-42** at 1:200,000 scale. The cartogram is subdivided into named/numbered map sheet blocks arranged in a grid, including:

- **Upper row (N-series):** XXXI, XXXII, XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV
- **Middle rows (M-series):** Sheets labeled with Roman numerals: **I, II, III, IV, V** (upper middle band) and **VII, VIII, IX, X, XI** (lower middle band)
- **Lower rows:** **XIII, XIV, XV, XVI, XVII**

Latitude/longitude grid lines are marked along the margins with "**N M**" annotations on both left and right sides, indicating the boundary between the N-42 and M-42 sheet series.

Numerous numerical annotations are visible within each sheet block, representing **report/work numbers** (e.g., 329, 363, 401, 409, 814, 1075, 2001, 3000, 1800, 864, 555, 238, 700, 828, etc.), corresponding to individual geological survey works referenced in the accompanying table.

---

## Key Content

The cartogram uses **three types of boundary lines** (as shown in the legend — Условные обозначения) to distinguish survey coverage at different scales:

1. **Solid thick lines** — Съёмки м-ба 1:200,000 (surveys at 1:200,000 scale)
2. **Dashed lines** — Съёмки м-ба 1:50,000 (surveys at 1:50,000 scale)
3. **Dotted lines** — Съёмки м-ба 1:10,000–2,000 (detailed surveys at 1:10,000 to 1:2,000 scale)

Additionally, a **shaded/hatched area** is indicated in the legend as:
- **Площадь геологического доизучения м-ба 1:200,000 Западно-Тенизской партии** — the area of additional/supplementary 1:200,000 geological study by the **West-Teniz Party** (the surveying team that produced Report 36572).

The irregular, stepped polygonal outlines drawn in bold lines delineate the **actual boundaries of each survey work**, which do not always align neatly with the standard sheet grid — indicating that many surveys covered partial sheets or cross sheet boundaries. The densest concentration of survey work numbers appears in the central and lower-central portions of the map (sheets II, III, VIII, IX, XIV, XV), suggesting these areas received the most intensive prior investigation. The peripheral sheets (XXXI, XXXV, XI, XVII, XIII) have fewer numbered works, indicating sparser prior coverage.

---
